title: Module 1
content:
DC Biasing in BJTs: Operating point and DC load line. Fixed, emitter
stabilized and voltage divider biasing. Bias stabilization. BJT Amplifiers:
RC coupled amplifier (CE configuration) – need of various components and
design. Small signal analysis: re and hybrid equivalent models for CE
configuration-small signal voltage gain, input and output impedance of CE
configuration

title: Module 2
content:
Junction Field Effect Transistors and MOSFETs: Structure, principle of
operation and characteristics of JFETs. Structure and physical operation &
characteristics of Enhancement and depletion type MOSFETs. MOSFET
amplifier circuits: Biasing in MOSFET amplifier-Single stage MOSFET
amplifier Circuit (CS configuration)-Design.

title: Module 3
content:
Power amplifiers: classification, class A and Class B power amplifier,
Class AB, Class C and Class D Power Amplifiers efficiency and distortion
(no analysis is required). Feed-back amplifiers: General feedback
structure, properties of negative feedback, and four basic feedback
topologies. Sinusoidal oscillators: criterion for oscillation, basic principles
of RC and crystal oscillators.

title: Module 4
content:
Pulse Circuits: Pulse shaping using RC circuits- Differentiating and
integrating circuits–applications. Multivibrators: astable, monostable and
bistable circuits – applications. Differential amplifiers: BJT differential
pair, operation with Common-Mode and differential mode input voltages,
CMRR
